news.Title: 台股強彈逾千點！兆元宴有吃有差 英業達、緯創噴漲停
news.Content:
輝達執行長黃仁勳訪台，並舉辦「兆元宴」宴請台灣核心供應鏈夥伴。其中包含台積電董事長魏哲家、廣達董事長林百里、廣達副董事長梁次震、聯發科執行長蔡力行、緯創董座林憲銘、華碩董事長施崇棠等約20位董座。

這場「黃仁勳風潮」也燒到台股，台股今（29）日早盤開高震盪，上漲930點，來到44532點，漲幅2.05%，預估成交量來到1.53兆。櫃買指數則來到443.77點，漲11.49點，漲幅2.66%。

權值股部分，台積電來到2340元，漲45元，漲幅1.96%，聯發科漲多拉回來到4380元，下跌30元，跌幅0.79%，鴻海則來到273元，上漲9.5元，漲幅3.8%。

此外，輝達核心供應鏈的股價也同步噴發，廣達來到323.5元，漲幅逾5%，華碩來到750元，漲幅逾8%，英業達、緯創雙雙衝漲停，技嘉、仁寶也大漲逾9%。

（封面圖／東森新聞）

news.Title: 台積電上漲55元　台股揚升逾千點攀高至44670點
news.Content:
（中央社記者張建中台北2026年5月29日電）美股主要指數28日全面收高，標普500與那斯達克指數收盤再創新高，台指期夜盤上揚950點，台股今天同步走高，早盤指數攀高至44670點，上漲1033.6點。

台積電美國存託憑證（ADR）上漲0.5%，台積電 (2330) 今天股價隨著走高，早盤攀高至2350元，上漲55元，市值達到新台幣60.94兆元，貢獻大盤約437點。

其餘權值股台達電 (2308) 、鴻海 (2317) 及廣達 (2382) 等也紛紛走高，電子類股指數上揚逾2%。傳統產業股水泥、塑膠、紡織、機電、電纜、玻璃、鋼鐵、汽車等類股也有不錯表現，類股指數上揚逾1%。

news.Title: 風評：台股衝上44000點　我們正在為後代種什麼樹？
news.Content: 一棵參天大樹能長成今天的模樣，從來不是 一朝一夕之功 ，而是數十年前有人在烈日下掘土、埋種、澆灌的結果。今天台灣引以為傲的半導體產業與AI供應鏈榮景，正是那一輩人在最艱困的年代裡，為我們種下的「乘涼樹」。我們今天所享有的經濟果實與國際地位，本質上是在收割四十年前那場豪賭的戰略紅利。 然而，當我們沉浸在「賣鏟子給淘金者」的財富狂歡中，台積電與供應鏈的獲利屢創新高時，一個嚴肅的問題正盤旋在我們頭頂：在AI算力指數成長、社會結構劇烈變動的今天，我們這代人正在為後代種下什麼樣的樹？或者說，我們現在難道只是在透支前人留下的養分，滿足於當下的安樂？ 1980年的憂患戰略 我們把時間拉回到1970、80年代，那是台灣近50年來，最動盪、最接近絕望的時刻。1971年台灣被迫退出聯合國，1979年台灣外部面臨台美斷交的國際孤立，內部則有美麗島事件後的社會張力。當時，美國第七艦隊不再協防台灣，守護台灣海峽，移民潮湧現，人心惶惶。 在那個「退無可退」的時間點，孫運璿、李國鼎等具備高度視野的政治家，展現了真正的「種樹精神」。他們不計個人榮辱，不陷入短視的政治算計，深知台灣的生存必須依賴一場徹底的產業換骨。 於是，1973年成立工研院，而當時工研院為引進半導體技術培育人才，1976年政府派出了一批年輕工程師赴美接受訓練，接著1980年竹科與聯電成立，到1985年李國鼎從美國延攬張忠謀回台灣，再到1987年台積電誕生，這幾乎是一場跨越半個世紀的戰略長征。 反觀今日，當政治人物忙著大撒幣，只關心任期內的選票與連任，只關心輝達訂單推升的 GDP 數據時，台灣已經在不知不覺中掉入了「太成功的陷阱」。我們擁有了最先進的「鏟子」，卻失去了當年那種「不計任期、只問國運」的戰略決心。 從數位鴻溝到AI斷層 當前AI的進化速度，早已脫離了人類習慣的線性增長。算力每三個月翻倍，數據透過算力的加持，不再只是冰冷的紀錄，而是成為具備「湧現能力」（Emergent Abilities）的預測模型。當「矽基智慧」（Silicon Brain）的準確性與預測力開始全面超越「生物智慧」（Bio-Brain），我們面臨的不僅是產業升級，更是社會階級的重組。 這將導致一場比「數位鴻溝」更殘酷的「AI 鴻溝」。能夠掌握並驅動AI的個人、企業與階級，將以指數級的速度累積財富與話語權；而不具備這種能力的群體，將在精準預測的演算法下，被永久性地排除在競爭圈外。 令人憂心的是，當這道社會斷層正在急劇擴大時，執政者的思維卻似乎在往反方向走。 同溫層政治的自殘 面對AI時代的劇烈變革，有責任感的政治家應該要致力於「縫合」，讓不同行業、不同階級的人能夠共同面對未知的風暴。然而，目前的現狀卻是日益嚴重的社會分化。 如果政治人物為了鞏固權力，利用AI演算法強化的同溫層效應，進行情緒性的切割與分化。將不同立場者標籤化、甚至動不動就用排他性的語言來鞏固基本盤。這種「切割納稅選民」的治理方式，對於正處於轉型關鍵期的國家而言，無疑是自殺行為。

news.Title: AI耗電激增　台積電：能源效率成晶片發展關鍵
news.Content:
（中央社阿姆斯特丹2026年5月28日綜合外電報導）台積電高層主管今天指出，人工智慧（AI）帶動的用電需求激增，已使能源效率而非運算能力，成為未來電腦晶片發展的主要限制因素。

路透社報導，台積電全球業務資深副總經理張曉強表示，從智慧手機到AI資料中心等領域，客戶如今愈來愈重視「在不增加耗電下提升效能」，因為營運商正面臨電力成本與供電可得性的壓力。

張曉強在荷蘭阿姆斯特丹（Amsterdam）一場會議中對媒體表示：「客戶目前最希望改善的是在能源效率方面。無論是邊緣運算、智慧手機、行動裝置、物聯網應用，還是高效能 AI資料中心都是如此。」

這項轉變也象徵半導體產業正來到重要轉折點。過去單純在晶片上塞入更多電晶體來提升效能的方式，已不足以支撐耗能驚人的AI工作量需求。

全球晶圓代工龍頭台積電替輝達（Nvidia）與超微（AMD）生產AI晶片，也為谷歌（Google）、亞馬遜（Amazon）、微軟（Microsoft）及Meta等大型雲端公司代工客製化AI處理器。

張曉強表示，提高電晶體密度仍是台積電技術藍圖核心，但先進封裝、晶片堆疊以及光子技術等其他方案正變得愈來愈重要，以提升效率。

他指出，台積電預估，從目前2奈米製程到預計2028年左右推出的A14製程世代，晶片耗電量可降低最多達30%，同時運算效能提升20%以上。

張曉強這番談話之際，台積電競爭對手也正積極探索其他提升晶片效能的方法。

中國競爭對手華為本週公布「Tau Scaling Law（韜（τ）定律」計畫，希望透過加快晶片內部資料移動速度來提升效能。

對此，張曉強表示：「這個概念在業界其實已存在相當長的時間。」

他認為，這大多仍仰賴更緊密整合元件，例如透過3D堆疊技術。

華為的做法反映出中國企業目前面臨的限制。由於美國主導的出口管制，中國企業無法取得荷蘭艾司摩爾（ASML）製造的極紫外光（EUV）微影設備，這類設備是製造更小電路的重要工具。

台積電是艾司摩爾極紫外光設備的大客戶，今年4月表示將延後數年導入下一代極紫外光技術。這也凸顯，相較於單純追求更微小電路設計，提升能源效率的設計對次世代AI晶片而言更加迫切。

news.Title: 美三大指數創新高！台股早盤漲破千點　台積電開2340元、上漲45元
news.Content:
即時中心／綜合報導

傳出美國和伊朗已就延長停火協議60天達成初步共識，同時投資者也在消化關鍵的通膨數據，美股三大指數週四（28日）再度同創歷史新高；台指期盤後也大漲950點、2.17％，收在44,789點。台股今（29）日開盤上漲179.04點、以43,815.48點開出，隨即持續衝高，目前指數上漲1033.6點、來到44,670.04點。台積電（2330）開盤上漲45元、以2,340元開出。

電子權值股方面，鴻海（2317）開269元，漲6元；聯發科（2454）以4,460元，漲50元；廣達（2382）開315.5元，漲7元；台達電（2308）以2,470元開出，上漲80元；日月光投控（3711）漲15元，開在642元；聯電（2303）開149元，漲7元；大立光（3008）漲90元，以3,515元開出。

金融方面，富邦金（2881）開110.5元，漲0.5元；國泰金（2882）開85.6元，上漲0.8元；中信金（2891）漲0.1元，開59.4元；兆豐金（2886）上漲0.65元，開40.45元；華南金（2880）開29.95元，漲0.35元；第一金（2892）開27.3元，小漲0.05元；合庫金（5880）以平盤22.7元開出。

《民視新聞網》提醒您：內容僅供參考，投資人於決策時應審慎評估風險，並就投資結果自行負責。

原文出處： 快新聞／美三大指數創新高！台股早盤漲破千點 台積電開2340元、上漲45元

news.Title: 美伊停火見曙光 台股暴漲逾千點 台積電狂噴
news.Content:
美國和伊朗談判代表可望達成延長停火協議，協議內容包括將停火再延長60天，激勵標普500與那指收盤時雙雙創下歷史新高，科技股重回領漲行列，台股今（29）日開盤大漲逾千點，最高來到44670.04點。

台積電大漲55元至2350元，權值股重回漲勢，老AI回神，鴻海漲逾3%，緯創漲停。記憶體股回神強漲，南亞科、華邦電漲逾6%。

台股昨(28) 日巨震逾1700點，終場收43636.44點，下跌620.36點或1.4%，守住5日均線，盤中振幅達1717.73點，爆出成交天量1.59兆元，外資賣超381.37億元，投信賣超62.06億元，自營商賣超150.38億元。

美股道瓊上漲24.69點或0.05%，收50,668.97點。S&P500上漲43.27點或0.58%，收7,563.63點。NASDAQ上漲242.74點或0.91%，收26,917.47點。費城半導體上漲126.96點或1.00%，收12,829.14點。

news.Title: 台股早盤漲逾千點 台積電漲55元
news.Content:
美股主要指數28日全面收高，標普500與那斯達克指數收盤再創新高。台北股市今天（29日）開盤漲179.04點，加權股價指數為43815.48點，成交金額新台幣162.50億元。開盤2分鐘後，漲1033.6點，早盤加權指數為44670.04點。 台積電 漲55元。

台積電今天股價隨著走高，早盤攀高至2350元，上漲55元，市值達到新台幣60.94兆元，貢獻大盤約437點。 其餘權值股台達電、鴻海及廣達等也紛紛走高，電子類股指數上揚逾2%。

傳統產業股水泥、塑膠、紡織、機電、電纜、玻璃、鋼鐵、汽車等類股也有不錯表現，類股指數上揚逾1%。

台股昨天收在43636.44點，美股四大指數終場道瓊工業指數上漲24.69點，漲幅0.05%。標普500指數上漲43.27點，漲幅0.58%，收在7563.63點。那斯達克指數上漲242.74點，漲幅0.91%，收在26917.47點。費城半導體指數上揚126.96點，漲幅1%。AI晶片龍頭輝達（NVDA-US）上漲0.78%、台積電ADR上漲0.48%。

news.Title: 智寶AI動畫助手AIFRED導入聯發科技術，搶食全球動畫商機
news.Content:
【財訊快報／記者戴海茜報導】近年隨著動畫、遊戲屢創現象級熱潮，智寶國際(7824)AI動畫助手AIFRED導入聯發科技(2454)LoRA Fusion技術，能直接在PC或行動裝置上執行，以文生圖，快速生成幾乎無損的圖像，搶食全球動畫市場商機。據研調公司Global View Research針對2018至2030年全球動畫市場分析報告，預計2030年全球動畫市場規模將達600.7億美元，且2023年至2030年年均複合成長率(CAGR)達9.8%。

智寶指出，AIFRED運用聯發科技裝置端生成式AI（GenAI）技術LoRA Fusion，結合智寶國際ACG內容IP，讓角色產生過程更加智慧；AIFRED亦結合中雍科技AI MEMC 動態補償技術，有效提高動畫師生產力；更將搭配JP GAMES的PWK開發套件，快速從圖生成3D角色。

智寶指出，結合聯發科技GenAI Security加密技術，AIFRED能夠根據IP需求，在具有認證的裝置上生成具有特定特徵的角色。不僅提升創作的靈活性，更在版權保護方面邁出重要的一步。未經認證的裝置將無法使用此功能，進而確保了IP的安全，防止未經授權的複製和分發，展現「智寶國際」在保護創作者權益方面的決心和能力。

news.Title: 64年一遇的大行情！ 杜金龍：波浪理論已被改寫
news.Content:
台股正迎來64年第二次罕見行情，強勁動能正改寫波浪理論。在融資餘額逼近5000億泡沫警戒之際，杜金龍建議透過「壓力測試」挑選大盤重挫時仍逆勢量增的標的，守住實質防線。

資金束縛解開後的輪動訊號

台股老仙覺杜金龍在臉書發文表示，面對這64年來第二次的罕見大行情，波浪理論的劇本也正在被市場強勁的動能改寫。他特別關注金管會放寬主動式ETF單一持股上限至25%的政策影響，這項變革促使台積電衝上2345元歷史天價。杜金龍指出，過去漲幅最大的是被迫分散資金的AI供應鏈與中小型股，如今資金解開束縛，究竟是全數回流台積電，還是繼續留在績效亮眼的AI概念股，將是接下來要密切追蹤的輪動訊號。

融資餘額逼近網路泡沫紀錄

在主動式ETF引發的良性循環下，市場情緒極度熱絡，但也伴隨隱憂。杜金龍觀察到目前大盤融資餘額已突破5000億，正逼近2000年網路泡沫時的歷史紀錄。他在文中提醒投資人，在市場全面樂觀之際，千萬不要讓過高的槓桿替你做決定。目前資金正轉向尋求「低基期補漲」目標，160元以下的股票已成為市場焦點。

透過壓力測試挑選實質防線

針對下半年的盤勢防禦，杜金龍提出了一套實戰篩選標準。他建議大家透過壓力測試，挑選在上次大盤5天重挫2000點時，能展現「逆勢上漲且成交量放大」的標的。杜金龍認為，這種在極端行情中依然屹立不搖的標的，才是投資人在這波史詩級行情中真正的實質防線。

（封面圖／翻攝自杜金龍臉書）

news.Title: Alphabet (GOOGL): The Best Strong Buy Stock to Buy and Hold for the Next 5 Years
news.Content:
Alphabet Inc. (NASDAQ:GOOGL) is one of the

12 Strong Buy Stocks to Buy and Hold for the Next 5 Years .

On May 20, 2026, Alphabet Inc. (NASDAQ:GOOGL) said it is deepening its presence in Missouri with a new data center in Montgomery County. The company said it is expanding infrastructure “responsibly,” while advancing energy affordability and creating new career pathways. Through its Capacity Commitment Framework agreement with Ameren, Alphabet said it is supporting the development of more than 500 megawatts of additional capacity and establishing a $20 million Energy Impact Fund to help lower utility bills through home weatherization and efficiency initiatives.

Also on May 20, Morgan Stanley said I/O 2026 showcased new, more efficient models and agentic offerings across commerce, travel, and daily life. The firm noted that Google introduced Gemini Spark, a personal agent that will roll out across the Gemini app, which now has more than 900M monthly active users, as well as Search, Gmail, Google Calendar, Google Drive, and other Google apps. Morgan Stanley kept an Overweight rating and $375 price target on Alphabet shares, saying “agentic sparks are set to fly with GOOGL lighting the torch.”

Photo by Firmbee.com on Unsplash

Wells Fargo also raised the firm’s price target on Alphabet to $435 from $427 and maintained an Overweight rating on the shares, citing improving market confidence in companies monetizing compute investments through cloud businesses.

On May 19, 2026, Google introduced Gemini 3.5, its latest family of models “combining frontier intelligence with action.” The company said 3.5 Flash is available through the Gemini app, AI Mode in Google Search, Google Antigravity, Gemini API in Google AI Studio and Android Studio, and Gemini Enterprise products. Google also said it is working on 3.5 Pro, which is already being used internally and is expected to start rolling out next month.

Alphabet Inc. (NASDAQ:GOOGL) offers various products and platforms across the United States, Europe, the Middle East, Africa, the Asia-Pacific, Canada, and Latin America.

The companies said the collaboration is aimed at helping manufacturers modernize operations, scale execution, and use agentic AI without the cost, disruption, time, and staffing often tied to traditional ERP. news.Title: Raymond James Raises its Price Target on Analog Devices (ADI)
news.Content:
Analog Devices, Inc. (NASDAQ:ADI) is one of the

12 Strong Buy Stocks to Buy and Hold for the Next 5 Years .

On May 21, 2026, Raymond James raised the firm’s price target on Analog Devices, Inc. (NASDAQ:ADI) to $430 from $385 and maintained an Outperform rating on the shares. Raymond James said Analog Devices delivered another strong quarter, with accelerating AI-driven data center demand accounting for most of the communications revenue. The firm also cited the acquisition of Empower Semiconductor, which expands the company’s AI and data center power exposure.

Posonskyi Andrey/Shutterstock.com

TD Cowen also raised the firm’s price target on Analog Devices, Inc. (NASDAQ:ADI) to $450 from $400 and maintained a Buy rating on the shares. TD Cowen said the upcycle continues, with strong results in industrial and communications supported by upside in automotive. The firm added that AI data center-related revenue grew considerably again, while the Empower tuck-in deal should bolster low-voltage power offerings.

On May 20, 2026, Analog Devices, Inc. (NASDAQ:ADI) reported fiscal Q2 adjusted EPS of $3.09, ahead of the consensus estimate of $2.90. Revenue totaled $3.62B, above the consensus estimate of $3.51B. CEO and Chair Vincent Roche said the quarter came in above the high end of the company’s outlook, reflecting “record demand” and operational discipline. Roche added that ADI continues to invest in technology leadership and long-term value for customers and shareholders.

Analog Devices, Inc. (NASDAQ:ADI) designs, manufactures, tests, and markets integrated circuits, software, and subsystem products globally.

news.Title: Here’s Why Microsoft (MSFT) is Among the 12 Strong Buy Stocks to Buy and Hold for the Next 5 Years
news.Content:
Microsoft Corporation (NASDAQ:MSFT) is one of the

12 Strong Buy Stocks to Buy and Hold for the Next 5 Years .

On May 22, 2026, Microsoft Corporation (NASDAQ:MSFT) agreed to pay $250M to settle investor litigation over its takeover of Activision Blizzard, Bloomberg’s Mike Leonard reported, citing court filings. The pact, filed in Delaware’s Chancery Court, would settle allegations that Activision ex-CEO Bobby Kotick and other board members offered a heavily discounted transaction to escape the sexual misconduct scandal that had engulfed the “Call of Duty” maker. Leonard noted that a judge allowed the case to move forward against Activision Blizzard last October, though claims that Microsoft exploited the scandal to push down the purchase price were dismissed.

dennizn/Shutterstock.com

On the same day, Business Insider’s Ashley Stewart reported that Microsoft’s chief marketing officer, Yusuf Mehdi, is set to depart after the next fiscal year, according to an internal memo. Mehdi, a 35-year company veteran, wrote that the “time is right” to begin planning for his next move and said he will work through the next fiscal year to help reimagine Windows for the agentic era, grow Microsoft 365 services, and advance the company’s One Copilot vision.

Earlier in May, Bill Ackman’s Pershing Square disclosed in an SEC filing its holdings as of March 31. Pershing Square took a new 5.65M-share stake in Microsoft (MSFT) during Q1.

Microsoft Corporation (NASDAQ:MSFT) develops and supports software, services, devices, and solutions worldwide.

news.Content:

儘管美伊多變的消息面干擾盤面，然6月起COMPUTEX展等科技盛宴陸續登場，另外景氣對策燈號連5紅，加上美方落實台美投資MOU降低傳產業關稅等基本面利多，可望帶動台股持續挑戰新高。

news.Title: 【國票金控晨訊】留意板卡布局機會
news.Content:
日期：2026年 5月29日

※操作建議與結論

◎今日盤勢及未來走勢

留意板卡布局機會

週四中東真假消息主導市場情緒，影響國際股市、債市、原油輪番承壓與反

彈，所幸當前進展顯示停火方向並未改變，帶動四大指數全數收漲，引領台指期

擺脫日盤的頹勢，台指期夜盤上漲950點(+2.17%)，台積電期貨夜盤亦有50元

(+2.16%)的漲幅，故預期台股早盤開出後有望朝昨日高點向上邁進。

◎短線觀點

台股雖以大量K黑作收，難免有高檔轉折之疑慮，然此次收跌屬非戰之罪，

且若能藉此利空順勢收斂不斷放大之正乖離率，反倒進一步鞏固中長期多頭架構

，後續若能將昨日低點43236點翻轉為支撐，多頭買盤亦能藉機換手再攻，配合

地緣政治和緩趨勢不變，台股往45000點關卡邁進應不是過分要求。

◎操作看法

儘管中東戰火仍有些許變數，然觀察過往科技發展從不因國際衝突停下腳步

，故反可趁盤勢震盪之際於投組內行汰弱留強，另考量Computex將於下週開幕，

屆時台股AI、半導體、伺服器等主軸有望成為成交重心，相關個股可留意布局機

◎重點產業與族群

板卡廠–2357華碩、2376技嘉、2377微星

產業觀點：AI時代板卡廠積極布局伺服器業務，除為基本面增添第二成長動

能之外，亦能擺脫過往受消費性電子淡旺季干擾的影響，整體營

運持續穩健向上。

news.Title: 友達ADR28日上漲0.02美元漲幅0.29%折台股21.43元
【統一證券晨訊】台股持續挑戰新高
news.Content:
日期：2026年 5月29日

※盤勢分析

美伊消息面干擾，台股基本面與科技盛宴共振，持續挑戰新高

◎昨日盤勢

受惠台積電下半年將調漲3奈米代工價的利多激勵下，週四台積電開盤股價

再創新高也同步帶動台股加權指數再創史高。然盤中傳出美伊再度互相開火的利

空影響，盤勢瞬間出現豬羊變色，盤中從高點到低點下殺逾1700點，一度失守5

日線，所幸尾盤出現低接買盤拉抬，指數跌幅收斂，收盤守穩5日線之上。儘管

指數劇烈震盪，指數下跌，但盤面資金仍維持輪動，其中受惠台美投資MOU非半

導體232關稅優惠下降15%的汽車零組件族群表現最為強勢。權值股方面，台積電

下跌0.22%，鴻海下跌0.38%，聯發科下跌4.96%，廣達下跌1.12%，台達電下跌

5.16%。

盤面強勢股，東陽、堤維西、耿鼎、時碩工業、帝寶、台達化、國喬、南茂

、凱美、立隆電、鈞寶、晶技、台半、萬潤等67檔個股漲停；亞崴、麗正、長科

*、昇陽半導體、微矽電子-創、華新科等漲幅8%以上；盤面弱勢股，森崴能源、

環宇-KY、高力、創意、東典光電、睿生光電、麗嬰房等跌停；日電茂、GIS-KY

、達航科技、直得、天二科技等跌幅9%以上。終場台灣加權指數上漲620.36點，

43636.44點作收，成交量為1.65兆元。觀察盤面變化，三大類股同步下跌，電子

下跌1.54%、傳產下跌0.88%、金融下跌0.49%。在次族群部份以數位雲端、紡織

纖維、汽車表現較佳，分別上漲4.44%、2.95%、1.31%。

◎資金動向

三大法人合計賣超604.81億元。其中外資賣超386.83億元，投信賣超67.59

億元，自營商賣超150.38億元。外資買超前五大為力積電、旺宏、南亞科、富邦

金、英業達；賣超前五大為群創、台新新光金、華邦電、華南金、華通。投信買

超前五大為台新新光金、華南金、長榮航、廣達、長榮；賣超前五大為聯電、富

邦金、遠東新、國泰金、玉山金。資券變化方面，融資減12.4億元，融資餘額為

5350.3億元，融券增0.65萬張，融券餘額為24.81萬張。外資台指期部位，空單

增加545口，淨空單部位58196口，借券賣出金額448.54億元。類股成交比重：電

子88.99%、傳產8.21%、金融2.80%。

◎今日盤勢分析

美國4月PCE指數年增3.8%，儘管符合預期，但仍創2023年5 月以來最大升幅

，整體通膨壓力依舊偏高，也強化市場對聯準會將長時間維持高利率的預期；美

國商務部公布美國第一季GDP季增年率修正值為1.6%，低於市場預期，市場預期

第二季經濟動能恐進一步放緩；美國上周初領失業救濟金人數小幅增加，但整體

仍維持在歷史低檔附近，顯示美國就業市場依舊維持一定韌性，美國昨公布經濟

數據好壞參半；中東消息面，儘管美伊雙方在荷姆茲海峽附近爆發軍事衝突，但

美媒報導，美國與伊朗談判代表已就一份為期 60天的停火與核談判備忘錄達成

初步共識，市場避險情緒降溫，然美財長隨後又稱川普總統不會接受該糟糕的協

議。中東消息面多變，但市場聚焦AI基本面，美股四大指數上漲，台積電ADR上

漲0.5%，台指期夜盤上漲950點，有利於台股然今日持續挑戰創新高。

台股後市分析如下：

第一、美國政府預計近日將發布聯邦公報，正式公告台美投資MOU中所載明

給予我國的非半導體232關稅優惠措施從今年5月1日起生效，其中包含「汽車零

組件以及原木、木材及木製衍生品稅率不高於15%；航空器零組件中鋼、鋁、銅

衍生品免除232關稅」。美方落實MOU中所載明的非半導體232關稅優惠措施，將

有助提升我國相關產業海外競爭力，未來得以進一步擴大布局美國市場。

第二、國發會28日公布最新4月景氣對策信號綜合判斷分數為39分，與上月

持平，燈號續亮出第5顆紅燈；國發會表示，製造業營業氣候測驗點受惠AI需求

強勁、中東地緣政治風險影響淡化，廠商對未來景氣看法回升。

第三、技術面觀察，周四大盤收上影線的黑K棒，收盤仍守穩5日線之上。技

術指標KD即將下彎、RSI往下、MACD柱體小幅放大，整體技術面為高檔震盪盤堅

，短線上收盤5日線未有效跌破且下彎前，盤勢仍有利於多方。

第四、盤面資金焦聚汽車零組件、被動元件、小型塑化等族群，包括：東陽

、堤維西、凱美、立隆電、台達化、國喬等表現強勢。

◎投資策略

儘管美伊多變的消息面干擾盤面，然6月起COMPUTEX展等科技盛宴陸續登場

，另外景氣對策燈號連5紅，加上美方落實台美投資MOU降低傳產業關稅等基本面

利多，可望帶動台股持續挑戰新高。惟季線正乖離來到歷史高位、外資期貨淨空

單逾5.8萬口，短線須提防行情劇烈波動。操作上可保持適度的資金彈性，建議

分批布局AI權值股。長線追蹤族群：台積電及先進製程/封裝、高速傳輸(含光傳

輸)、載板與PCB、機箱滑軌、被動元件、功率半導體、航太衛星、特用化學及漲

價概念等族群。

news.Title: 中華電信ADR28日下跌0.23美元跌幅0.53%折台股136.74元
聯電ADR28日上漲0.46美元漲幅2.07%折台股142.56元
台積電ADR28日上漲2.13美元漲幅0.50%折台股2670.50元
1分鐘讀財經》台指期夜盤狂飆950點！台積電期貨驚噴新天價 今跳空開高「必看5大重點」
news.Content:
小編今天（29）日精選5件國內外重要財經大事。台指期夜盤昨上漲950點，台積電期貨盤後上漲50元，台股今（29）日開盤預期跳空開高。法人分析，受海外市場科技股反彈帶動，市場資金重新回流權值股，台股後市觀盤需留意五大重點。

台股後市五大觀盤重點：今日盤後MSCI權重調升生效，晶圓代工龍頭預期引領被動資金撐盤。基本面受惠瑞銀看好AI、雲端業者資本支出年增73%，帶動記憶體產業向上循環。雖然面臨中東局勢、通膨及不降息等總經利空，但非基本面下殺屬估值修正，可擇優布局。短線防守10日線、月線及前低40,020點，後續則有6月初COMPUTEX與輝達論壇等利多題材支撐。

【1】台股巨震1,717點，台積電鉅額交易再現天價

展望台股後市，第一金投顧董事長黃詣庭指出，短線市場面臨中東局勢升溫、通膨疑慮，但消息面正向訊息仍偏多，基本面無須過度悲觀，短線可觀察10日線至月線支撐區，若情勢進一步惡化，則須留意40,020點不可失守。

【2】黃仁勳兆元宴 台鏈大點兵

輝達（NVIDIA）執行長黃仁勳繼日前密集會晤供應鏈夥伴後，28日晚間備受矚目的「兆元宴」正式登場。這場餐敘不僅是台灣科技業重量級領袖聚首，更成為AI供應鏈市值重估的最佳縮影，甚至被外界視為行走版「0050」。

【3】和碩AI伺服器業務 今年拚增10倍

和碩28日召開股東會，共同執行長鄧國彥看好今年AI伺服器業務與去年相比會有10倍數成長，上半年還在調整階段，下半年開始會有不錯的成果顯現。共同執行長鄭光志也透露，因應客戶需求，下半年會投入更多的生產人力與空間，尤其是人力方面可能還要增加3～4倍，才有望在「秋收」的季節裡獲得10倍數成長。

【4】降稅利多 汽零族群歡呼

川普萬萬稅終有解，汽車零組件廠迎來輸美關稅調降的重磅利多，28日上演慶祝行情，東陽（1319）、昭輝（1339）等11檔刷一排漲停，無懼大盤劇烈震盪，多頭買單高掛，成功帶動上市汽車類股逆勢上漲1.31％，是台股吸金主軸。

【5】融資換股權 川普力捧無人機

外媒引述消息報導，川普政府正與多家無人機公司洽談融資合作，甚至可能以聯邦資金換取企業股權，目的是提升本土產能並降低成本。這些企業包括：曾獲陸軍偵察無人機合約的Performance Drone Works、川普長子擔任顧問兼股東的無人機零件供應商Unusual Machine，以及由紅杉資本支持、專注研發第一人稱視角無人機的新創公司Neros Technologies。

news.Title: 【盤前焦點】美股收高　法人：台股挑戰44000點關卡
news.Content:
（中央社記者張建中台北2026年5月29日電）美股主要指數28日全面收高，標普500與那斯達克指數收盤再創新高。法人表示，台指期夜盤上揚950點，台股今天可能挑戰44000點關卡。

在科技股領漲下，美股主要指數收高。道瓊工業指數上漲24.69點，漲幅0.05%。標普500指數上漲43.27點，漲幅0.58%，收在7563.63點。那斯達克指數上漲242.74點，漲幅0.91%，收在26917.47點。費城半導體指數上揚126.96點，漲幅1%。

台股28日上攻至44954點歷史新高後，指數急殺最低下探43236點，高低震盪達1717點；終場收43636.44點，下跌620.36點，跌幅1.4%。

國內外產業訊息方面，美國於美東時間28日上午在聯邦公報正式公告對台灣的非半導體232關稅優惠措施，包含汽車零組件輸美稅率降至15%。

HDI廠華通 (2313) 表示，目前華通衛星板的產出已是世界第一，將擴大衛星通訊與太空板優勢，看好「太空PCB的需求不可限量」，華通會爭取更多光通訊相關訂單；隨AI基礎建設驅動PCB產業鏈升級，集團明、後年營收、利潤都將有大幅成長。

電機廠東元 (1504) 指出，在台灣和東南亞積極布局人工智慧資料中心（AIDC）應用，持續開發北美市場，預估今年底AIDC營收占東元電力能源事業群比重將超過30%，未來可以達到50%。

半導體封測材料商利機 (3444) 宣布，董事會決議投資明鈞源精微科技約新台幣5.08億元，預計7月1日交割，完成後利機對明鈞源持股比率由6%提升至82%，明鈞源將成利機集團具有實質經營權的子公司，強化利機在人工智慧AI散熱領域布局。

長榮海運 (2603) 表示，第2季燃油成本將高於第1季，目前已與主要油商簽定下半年供油合約，對第2季及第3季市場維持樂觀看法，第3季為歐美市場傳統旺季，短期內紅海航線仍難恢復正常，供需結構預期相對平衡。

news.Title: 2 AI Infrastructure Stocks That Could Rise 25% and 80% Despite Overdone Spending Fears
news.Content:
Two of the hottest stocks in the market right now are Advanced Micro Devices (NASDAQ: AMD) and Micron (NASDAQ: MU) . And according to some Wall Street analysts, these stocks still have more room to run.

Baird analyst Tristan Gerra has a street-high $625 price target on AMD, up from $300 ahead of the company's early May earnings report, representing about 25% from here (as of May 26). Meanwhile, UBS analyst Timothy Arcuri just tripled his price target on Micron from $535 to $1,625, representing around 80% upside.

Will AI create the world's first trillionaire? Our team just released a report on the one little-known company, called an "Indispensable Monopoly" providing the critical technology Nvidia and Intel both need. Continue »

Let's take a look at why these two artificial intelligence (AI) stocks are so hot right now and whether or not it's too late to get in.

AMD: A huge inference and agentic AI opportunity

AMD is riding two of the most powerful trends in AI right now in inference and agentic AI. These markets are just starting to boom, and AMD is well-positioned to see explosive growth in the coming years as a result.

While rival Nvidia has dominated the AI model training market, helped by most foundational AI code being written on its CUDA software platform, inference is less technologically demanding and tends to be more memory-bound than compute-bound. With hyperscalers looking to diversify away from Nvidia and looking for all the compute power they can get their hands on, AMD is set to be a big beneficiary. Its chiplet design can pack in more high-bandwidth memory (HBM), making its graphics processing units (GPUs) well-suited for inference. Meanwhile, it has already signed large GPU deals worth over $100 billion each, which should be a huge growth driver in the years ahead.

At the same time, the company has a huge opportunity in the data center central processing unit (CPU) market due to the rise of agentic AI. CPUs act like the brains of a computer, and to manage AI agents, AI data centers are going to need a whole lot more high-power CPUs. While the GPU-to-CPU ratio for training was 8:1, it moves to 1:1 for agentic AI. AMD is one of the leaders in this space in a market that it expects to rise to over $120 billion in the next few years.

Given its huge growth opportunities ahead, AMD looks poised to continue having solid upside from here.

Micron: Rising from the memory supercycle

The rise of Micron has been nothing short of spectacular. The stock is up more than 800% over the past year, largely due to earnings growth, and it still trades at a forward price-to-earnings (P/E) ratio of just 8.6 times fiscal 2027 analyst estimates.

Story Continues

Micron is one of the big three dynamic random-access memory (DRAM) makers, along with Korean competitors SK Hynix and Samsung . Right now, the entire DRAM market is very undersupplied due to surging demand for HBM. GPUs and other AI chips need to be packaged with HBM to optimize their performance, and as noted above with AMD, inference tends to be much more memory-bound than compute-bound, which is just adding another tailwind.

With the big memory makers focused on HBM and this specialized form of DRAM requiring upwards of three times the wafer capacity as ordinary DRAM, overall prices have skyrocketed, which has led to huge sales growth and enormous margin expansion for Micron and its competitors. And while these companies are working to increase capacity, they are also constrained by the finite number of EUV and DUV machines that ASML can produce each year.

While the memory market has historically been very cyclical with large boom-and-bust cycles, the big three players have started to lock in three to five-year commitments for the first time, trading some near-term price for more long-term visibility. Together with high HBM growth, ASML machine bottlenecks, and a low valuation, Micron could have a lot more room to run.

news.Title: AvePoint Conference: AI Governance Push Fuels AgentPulse Pipeline
news.Content:
Key Points

Interested in AvePoint, Inc.? Here are five stocks we like better.

AI governance is becoming a major growth driver for AvePoint, with the company saying concerns about AI security, shadow AI, and agent oversight are boosting demand for its control suite. AgentPulse is already in more than half of the control-suite pipeline and could become a standalone product later.

AvePoint is broadening beyond Microsoft into a multi-cloud data management and AI security platform, with customer conversations now spanning Google, Salesforce, AWS, Azure and other environments. Executives said the company’s value is strongest in mixed-license, multi-platform deployments where AI risk and exposure are top concerns.

The company expects a second-half recovery in public sector demand , especially in U.S. federal, which was a headwind last year. Management also said future investment will focus mainly on sales, marketing and brand awareness as AvePoint works toward $1 billion in ARR.

AvePoint (NASDAQ:AVPT) executives said the company is broadening its position from its Microsoft roots into a wider data management, governance and AI security platform, with artificial intelligence driving new customer conversations and pipeline growth.

Speaking at a Jefferies event hosted by analyst Joe Gallo, AvePoint CEO Tianyi Jiang described the company’s evolution from serving on-premises Microsoft Exchange and SharePoint environments to supporting cloud-based Office workloads and, increasingly, multi-cloud deployments across Google, Salesforce, Azure, Google Cloud Platform and Amazon Web Services.

→ Rocket Lab Keeps Making Headlines and Highs—Here's What's Driving the Latest Move

Jiang said AvePoint’s historical focus on data management, curation and governance is becoming more relevant as enterprises deploy AI. He said customers and partners now view AvePoint as part of the AI infrastructure stack for “AI trust, AI governance, AI security.”

AI Governance Drives Control Suite Demand

Jiang said the push to deploy AI has made data quality, classification, access controls and security more important for customers. He said AvePoint is seeing concern around “shadow AI,” as enterprises seek visibility into which agents are being created, what data they access and how they interact with other agents.

→ Quantum Stocks Just Got a Lifeline—Who Benefits Most?

The company’s control suite currently contributes about 26% of annual recurring revenue, but Jiang said it represents about 40% of the company’s total pipeline. He added that more than 50% of the control pipeline now includes AgentPulse, AvePoint’s recently introduced product for managing AI agents.

Story Continues

“This is now top of mind for every C-level around AI security and AI risk,” Jiang said.

→ 5 Stocks Winning the AI Race While Everyone Watches NVIDIA

CFO Jim Caci said AgentPulse was introduced midway through the first quarter and is currently being sold in bundles with other control suite products. He said the product has helped increase uptake of those upper-level bundles in the pipeline. Based on normal deal cycles, Caci said the impact is more likely to show up in the third and fourth quarters than in the second quarter. He also said AvePoint may eventually introduce a standalone AgentPulse SKU.

Executives Say AI Is Broadening Conversations Beyond Microsoft

Jiang said AI is also changing how customers evaluate infrastructure. While AvePoint remains heavily connected to Microsoft, he said many enterprise conversations now focus less on Microsoft Copilot specifically and more on agentic deployments across multiple platforms.

He said AvePoint is agnostic about whether customers create agents through Microsoft, Claude, Gemini, Salesforce or other systems, and that customers increasingly frame the issue as “AI exposure” and “AI risk,” rather than only a Microsoft estate problem.

Jiang also addressed Microsoft Purview, describing Microsoft and Google as “market makers” and saying AvePoint has long operated alongside large platform providers. He said AvePoint can use Purview tags and classifications when customers have them, or apply machine learning-based tagging when they do not. He said AvePoint’s value is strongest in mixed-license and multi-cloud environments, where customers want to maximize returns across technology stacks.

Resiliency Remains Part of Broader Data Management

On AvePoint’s resiliency suite, Jiang said the company increasingly views resiliency as part of broader data management and data security rather than a standalone category. He said demand rose after an Amazon regional data center disruption in the Middle East, which prompted some customers to reassess reliance on hyperscalers for disaster recovery and redundancy.

Jiang said AvePoint touches about 1,000 petabytes of data each day through snapshots, creating a data store that can support governance, taxonomy management and analysis of unstructured data. He said customers are asking for ways to gain intelligence from data without affecting production environment performance.

Public Sector Expected to Recover

Caci said AvePoint’s public sector practice is global, with activity in Japan, Singapore and Europe as well as the United States. However, he said the U.S. federal business was a headwind last year and “didn’t really see any growth.”

This year, Caci said AvePoint is seeing “nice pipeline growth” and first-quarter performance in line with expectations. He said the federal business is typically stronger in the third and fourth quarters and that the company expects a recovery in public sector demand, particularly in U.S. federal, during those periods.

Caci said that recovery is one factor supporting AvePoint’s forecast for growth acceleration in the second half of the year.

Investment Focused on Sales, Marketing and Brand Awareness

Asked about guidance, including 26% constant-currency ARR growth, Caci said AvePoint’s visibility has improved through forecasting discipline, CRM systems and pipeline management. He said the company has spent years improving its methodology for reviewing opportunities, quota capacity and product needs.

Caci also discussed AvePoint’s plan to invest in 2026, saying the company has already built infrastructure that can support a much larger business. He said the company is approaching $450 million in ARR and has a global structure more typical of a company with $1 billion or more in revenue scale.

As a result, he said incremental investment will be concentrated in sales and marketing, including brand awareness and channel strategy, rather than large increases in overhead. He said AvePoint previously spent about 44% to 45% of revenue on sales and marketing when it went public, compared with about 31% in the latest quarter, with a long-term target of 30%. He said general and administrative expenses are targeted to decline toward 10% of revenue, while research and development is expected to remain around 12%.

Caci said those 2026 investments are not needed to hit the company’s ARR forecast for that year and are more likely to contribute in late 2026, 2027 and beyond.

Jiang said AvePoint is also using AI internally, including GitHub Copilot in development, content support in marketing, contract management in legal and finance applications. He said the goal is to grow toward $1 billion in ARR while maintaining profitability and improving efficiency, rather than adding costs proportionally with revenue growth.

About AvePoint (NASDAQ:AVPT)

AvePoint, Inc (NASDAQ:AVPT) is a leading software provider specializing in data management, governance, and compliance solutions for Microsoft 365 and related cloud platforms. Founded in 2001 and headquartered in Jersey City, New Jersey, the company offers a comprehensive suite of cloud-based and on-premises tools designed to help organizations migrate, manage, and protect their collaboration data. AvePoint's flagship Cloud Platform delivers backup, governance, reporting, and migration services for SharePoint, Teams, Exchange, OneDrive, and Salesforce environments.

With a customer base spanning thousands of organizations across more than 100 countries, AvePoint serves enterprises, government agencies, and educational institutions seeking to ensure data security, regulatory compliance, and operational resilience.

news.Title: Hut 8 director dumps $2 million in shares
news.Content:
The shares of Hut 8 Corp. (Nasdaq: HUT) have been on a rally of late.

The company which began as a Bitcoin (BTC) miner has been transitioning into a data infrastructure firm that can swing between artificial intelligence (AI) and mining operations.

Related: Michael Burry drops bombshell on SpaceX, OpenAI IPOs

While most Bitcoin miners are completely abandoning operations while pivoting to AI, Hut 8 is still attempting to strike a balance but is positioning itself less as a miner.

On May 6, the company announced a 15-year, $9.8 billion lease for a large-scale AI data centre project in Texas. Though the facility was intended for Bitcoin mining, it was repurposed for AI operations.

Hut 8 said NVIDIA (Nasdaq: NVDA) is its technology partner, with Phase 1 of the campus engineered to NVIDIA's DSX reference architecture for gigawatt-scale AI factories.

Following the announcement, the HUT stock's price surpassed the $100 mark on May 6.

Since then, the company's stock continued to hit fresh highs and even hit its all-time high (ATH) of $126.26 on May 28.

Popular on TheStreet Roundtable:

Cathie Wood sends strong prediction on Bitcoin

China, Japan, UAE, India sell billions in U.S. Treasuries

XRP traders withdraw millions of dollars in 24 hours

Hut 8 director Amy Marie Wilkinson sells shares

Amy Marie Wilkinson, a board director at Hut 8, filed a Form 4 with the Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) on May 26 and disclosed selling 20,000 shares at $100.78 apiece on May 21.

Though Wilkinson dumped $2,015,600 in HUT shares, she still owns 262,136 shares of the company.

Insider selling is a standard market practice in which a company executive or a major shareholder legally sells shares of their own company in the open market.

It is required by the SEC to report such sales to the securities regulator, including details such as the stock sale price, number of shares sold, and date of the sale.

Such sales shouldn't necessarily be read as an indication of low confidence among top leaders in the company's prospects. Executives sell their shares for a variety of purposes such as portfolio diversification, taxes, etc.

The Hut 8 stock closed at $124.24 on May 28.

Related: Veteran investor names the next 'NVIDIA' before AI boom

news.Title: 和碩股東會  童子賢：台股仍具基本面支撐尚未進入泡沫階段
news.Content: 和碩 董事長 童子賢 28日早上主持股東會到了傍晚就出現在兆元宴現場準備和輝達執行長黃仁勳共進晚餐。（圖片來源／ 信傳媒資料庫） 和碩周四（28）日召開股東會，面對全球消費性電子需求疲軟，去年營收略微下滑，每股盈餘5.39元。不過，會中向股東強調和碩已經成功實現NVIDIA GB300與HGX B300等高階AI伺服器，未來還要佈局機器人與車用領域。 和碩2025年合併營收1兆1172億元，比起2024年減少81億元、年減0.72％，EPS達5.39元；股東會承認配發股利4元。 台股頻頻創新高，已開始上攻4萬4千點，和碩的股價在4月漲了8％，這個月至今漲1.3％多來到83.4元，媒體採訪時問童子賢是否擔心AI泡沫化？台股過高？ 和碩董事長童子賢回應，今年第一季上市公司獲利約1兆6千多億元，若以全年估算並考量下半年旺季效應，目前台股本益比約落在22倍至28倍之間，「還沒有真正超過28倍」。 他說，從上市公司獲利與本益比來看，目前台股仍具基本面支撐，尚未進入泡沫階段。他也指出，台灣處在AI潮流中心，現階段發展連30%都不到，看好後續發展空間。 近日市場傳出輝達有意入股載板大廠景碩，讓27日才開完股東會的景碩又再獲關注。和碩透過子公司持股景碩合計25%以上，是單一最大股東，對此童子賢回應說，「我要澄清沒有入股，不論是從資本結構還是從法律用語角度來看，入股都是一件非常嚴謹的事。」 根據鏡周刊報導，童子賢表示「原來不是股東的人，要入股有2種方式，一種是到現貨市場買股票，但此舉對公司營運沒有幫助，」他強調，「如果要對公司有幫助就是要發行新股，完全由對方認購，這個才符合所謂的入股，而我要澄清並沒有這一件事！」 早上開股東會，傍晚童子賢就去參加黃仁勳的兆元宴了，酒水之間，可能也決定了和碩今年的營運方向。

news.Title: Delta Airlines Touts Amazon.com, Inc. (AMZN)’s in-Flight Wi-Fi Capabilities amid Starlink Tussle
news.Content:
Amazon.com, Inc. (NASDAQ: AMZN ) is one of the best 52-week high stocks to invest in, according to short sellers . On May 18, Delta Air Lines CEO Ed Bastian defended the company’s decision to settle on Amazon.com Inc. (NASDAQ:AMZN) to provide in-flight Wi-Fi service in its airplanes.

The airline settled on Amazon Leo service over SpaceX’s Starlink due to its lower costs and additional technology offerings. According to Bastian, Amazon offers more than just satellite technology, given the company’s strong retail capabilities, Amazon Prime, and Video gaming technologies. Currently, Starlink does not offer such capabilities.

In addition, Amazon Leo’s internet service offers much-improved bandwidth at a much lower price point than Starlink. Delta Air Lines is to install the Amazon internet service in 500 aircraft starting in 2028. Amazon is to offer customers high-speed, low-latency Wi-Fi from gate to gate. The decisions have already rattled Elon Musk, who has criticized the move that bypassed Starlink.

On May 18, Jefferies analysts highlighted Amazon as one of their highest‑conviction Buy‑rated stocks, pointing to its AWS unit, which they believe may be entering an early reacceleration phase as new capacity comes online and AI partnerships enhance longer‑term visibility.

Amazon.com, Inc. (NASDAQ:AMZN) is a multinational technology and e-commerce corporation that operates a massive global marketplace. Beyond retail, it functions as a major cloud computing provider, produces original entertainment, and manufactures consumer electronics.

While we acknowledge the potential of AMZN as an investment, we believe certain AI stocks offer greater upside potential and carry less downside risk. If you're looking for an extremely undervalued AI stock that also stands to benefit significantly from Trump-era tariffs and the onshoring trend, see our free report on the best short-term AI stock .

news.Title: Is This Why Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company Limited (TSM) is One of the Best 52-week High Stocks to Invest In?
news.Content:
Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company Limited (NYSE: TSM ) is one of the best 52-week high stocks to invest in, according to short sellers . On May 18, analysts at Bernstein SocGen Group reiterated an Outperform rating on Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company Limited (NYSE:TSM) and raised the price target to $430 from $351.

The price target hike comes amid confidence that the company is in a phase of robust growth. Consequently, the research firm expects the company to deliver a 28% compound annual earnings-per-share growth rate over the next 2.5 years. The robust growth would come as Bernstein SocGen does not expect a potential collaboration between Apple and Intel to have any significant impact on Taiwan Semiconductor‘s market position.

While the research firm expects Intel and Samsung Foundry to gain some business due to geopolitical factors, this will be limited. Whereas Samsung Foundry is improving its 4-nanometer and 2-nanometer processes, Taiwan Semiconductor remains ahead.

Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company Limited (NYSE:TSM) is the world’s largest dedicated semiconductor foundry. TSMC does not design or sell its own products. Instead, it manufactures microchips for over 500 tech companies—such as Apple, Nvidia, and AMD. TSMC mass produces its designs on silicon wafers.

While we acknowledge the potential of TSM as an investment, we believe certain AI stocks offer greater upside potential and carry less downside risk. news.Title: Australian watchdog sues Amazon unit over children's backpack safety
news.Content:
May 29 (Reuters) - Australia's competition watchdog said on Friday that it has initiated legal action against Amazon's local unit, alleging breaches ‌of product safety labelling laws related to children's backpacks.

According to ‌the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission (ACCC), the company is accused of supplying children's "Unicorn Toddler Backpacks" ​that were not compliant with mandatory safety standards for button batteries.

More from Yahoo Scout

What are the specific risks of button batteries to children?

What penalties is the ACCC seeking against Amazon Australia?

How many unsafe backpacks were sold through Amazon's platform?

What safety violations is Amazon Australia facing legal action for?

The ACCC alleges the backpacks were stored in Amazon Commercial Services' fulfilment centres in Australia between June 22 and November 1, 2022, without the required warning labels on ‌either the products or ⁠their outer packaging.

The ACCC says 41 backpacks were sold to Australian consumers via amazon.com.au during this period, with a ⁠further 267 units still held in Amazon's fulfilment centres as of November 1, 2022.

Amazon spokesperson said in a statement that it is "considering the case filed by ​the ACCC", ​and added that policies and controls are ​in place to make sure ‌the listed products meet compliance and safety measures.

"Unicorn Toddler Backpacks" were designed for children and included a detachable light-up unicorn plush toy containing button batteries, ACCC said in its statement.

The ACCC argues that by having possession and control of the goods through this service, Amazon AU is subject ‌to the same obligations under Australian Consumer ​Law as other suppliers.

"Button batteries pose a serious ​hazard for young children. ​If swallowed or inserted, they can cause severe internal burns ‌and injury, and in some cases ​death," ACCC Deputy ​Chair Catriona Lowe said.

The regulator is seeking declarations, penalties, costs and other orders from the Federal Court.

The ACCC said this was its ​first Federal Court case ‌against an online marketplace alleging breaches of mandatory product safety laws.

(Reporting ​by Rajasik Mukherjee and Aamir Sheik Khalid in Bengaluru; Editing ​by Shreya Biswas and Sherry Jacob-Phillips)

news.Title: Anthropic Just Delivered Spectacular News For Amazon and Alphabet
news.Content: It isn't quite David defeating Goliath, but Anthropic just did something few would have thought possible a few years ago. The company is now the most valuable AI start-up in the world, surpassing OpenAI with its latest funding round. The owner of the Claude chatbot just closed its Series H round, valuing the company at $965 billion. Anthropic raised $65 billion from investors, including ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Anthropic, an AI startup, has raised $65 billion in its latest funding round, valuing the company at $965 billion, surpassing OpenAI.
• Amazon (AMZN) and Alphabet (GOOGL) are significant investors in Anthropic, with Amazon investing $13 billion (with a further $20 billion pledged) and Alphabet investing up to $40 billion (on top of $13 billion already invested).
• Anthropic's revenue run rate reached $47 billion, and its new Claude Opus 4.8 model outperforms other publicly available AI platforms in benchmarks.
• The funding will be used to expand compute capacity, including significant cloud infrastructure spending with Amazon Web Services (AWS) and Google Cloud.
• Anthropic has pledged to spend $100 billion on AWS and $200 billion on Google Cloud over the next five to ten years.
news.Keywords: AI startup, venture capital, cloud infrastructure, strategic investment, valuation

news.Title: 台股和國際半導體股恐進入大震盪期，操作需要留意
news.Content:
【財訊快報／魏聖峰】台股開始進入大震盪行情，週四加權指數早盤創下44,954.09點的歷史新高，不久之後漲幅開始收斂，一直到11:30時指數翻黑並加速下探，盤中最深一度大跌1,020點，終場仍以相對低點作收。集中市場成交量爆出1.59兆元的大量，仍低於前一個交易日的1.62兆元。這波台北股市的漲勢是美國半導體股的大漲行情開始，起頭是Nvidia公布財報透露AI產業景氣沒有泡沫化，而是不斷地逐年增加投資量能，未來甚至可以達到3~4兆美元的年投資額。雖然財報公布後Nvidia本身股價沒有進一步大漲，卻引發費城半導體指數的大漲行情。美光、應用材料、博通、高通、ARM等指標性半導體股都大漲，墊高半導體股的市值。台積電ADR、聯電ADR、日月光投控ADR股價也沒有缺席這波半導體股行情。韓國KOSPI指數也在三星電子、海力士股價大漲激勵頻創新高。

但由於美國、南韓以及日本半導體相關股的短線漲幅已大，股價開始出現震盪，由於先前累積的潛在獲利了結賣壓不輕，未來市場進入大震盪的行情恐難免，未來每日震幅超過千點以上的行情將有可能常見。這段期間，台股的操作就要非常小心，不宜太早進場接手股票。

聯發科(2454)週四股價開盤就開低，股價一度下探4,270元，收盤時跌幅收斂到下跌近5%。這波聯發科股價漲幅很大，股價甚至比台積電(2330)高了將近一倍，累積的獲利回吐壓力不輕。創意(3443)早盤股價一度上漲，之後直線下跌股價被砍到跌停。M31(6643)、世芯-KY(3661)、矽力-KY(6415)、新唐(4919)、神盾(6462)的股價也都呈現5%以上的跌幅，這類短線大跌的股票，還需要觀察量縮止跌後才能回頭買進。不過，智原(3035)、太欣(5302)、晶焱(6411)、應廣(6716)等股價則出現落後補漲行情。

記憶體股出現兩樣情，南亞科(2408)、旺宏(2337)、晶豪科(3006)股價反彈，南亞科盤中股價一度大漲9%後回檔，終場上漲3.85%，並爆出大量。最近股價大漲的華邦電(2344)週四股價大跌7.1%，是跌幅最大的記憶體股。這也顯示漲多的記憶體股股價要進入休息震盪，或許週四上漲的記憶體股，週五股價可能也會進入震盪。

封測股也同樣進入震盪，日月光投控(3711)股價也從高點回檔，收盤僅下跌3.34%，跌幅不算多。先前漲幅很大且本益比已高的訊芯-KY(6451)週四股價下跌5.48%、探針卡大廠的雍智科技(6683)股價跌幅也有4.49%。封測股週四算是相對穩定的族群，京元電子(2449)、欣銓(3264)、華東(8110)、華泰(2329)、精材(3374)等股週四股價都維持漲勢，力成(6239)早盤股價一度攻上漲停板，收盤時漲幅收斂到僅上漲4.99%。像力成這檔股票週五可能會出現震盪，須留意操作。

台光電(2383)、智邦(2345)、臻鼎-KY(4958)、金像電(2368)、華通(2313)、奇鋐(3017)、光寶科(2301)、群創(3481)、南電(8046)、景碩(3189)、欣興(3037)、致茂(2360)、健策(3653)等股，週四股價跌幅不少，成交量也不小，這類股票也暫時不要亂接手。

news.Title: 《美股》美伊傳擬延長停火 軟體/無人機/那指嗨
news.Content:
MoneyDJ新聞 2026-05-29 07:03:07 郭妍希 發佈

市場謠傳美國、伊朗已達成將停火行動展延60天的草擬協議，再加上雲端軟體及資料倉儲服務商Snowflake Inc.暴漲推升軟體股，帶動道瓊工業平均指數、那斯達克指數、標準普爾500指數再創歷史收盤新高，投資人也持續消化關鍵通膨數據。

道瓊工業平均指數5月28日終場上漲0.05%(24.69點)、收50,668.97點，連續第2個交易日創歷史收盤新高。那斯達克指數上漲0.91%(242.74點)、收26,917.47點，連續第3個交易日創歷史收盤新高。標準普爾500指數上漲0.58%(43.27點)、收7,563.63點，連續第3個交易日創歷史收盤新高。費城半導體指數上漲1%(126.96點)、收12,829.14點。

路透社引述消息人士報導，展延停火的草擬協議仍需要美國總統川普(Donald Trump)的認可。與此同時，伊朗官媒塔斯尼姆通訊社(Tasnim)指出，與美方的潛在合作備忘錄(MOU)尚未完成或確認。

Axios也引述兩名美國官員與一名地方消息人士報導，美國與伊朗談判代表已對一份將停火期間延長60天的MOU達成共識，並進一步對伊朗的核武計畫進行商談。不過，川普尚未給予最終核可。

上述消息傳來後，油價漲幅應聲收斂。美國西德州中級(WTI)原油期貨28日終場小漲約0.3%、收在每桶88.90美元。國際布蘭特(Brent)原油期貨則下跌0.6%、收在每桶93.71美元。

軟體股跳高、帶動整體科技股走強。Snowflake 27日美股盤後 公布 的第一季度(2-4月)財報、Q2 (5-7月)財測皆擊敗分析師原先預期，還將2027會計年度的全年產品營收指引，從先前預估的56.6億美元上調至58.4億美元，代表多達31%的年增長率。另外，Snowflake並決定要對亞馬遜(Amazon.com)雲端服務投入60億美元。

Snowflake聞訊跳空暴漲36.48%、收239.20美元，創2025年12月3日以來收盤新高。其他軟體類股同步走強。微軟(Microsoft Corp.)、雲端工作流程自動化解決方案供應商ServiceNow、文件導向的資料庫管理系統開發商MongoDB分別勁揚3.47%、6.47%、10.6%。

iShares擴展科技軟體類股ETF (iShares Expanded Tech-Software Sector ETF，代號為IGV.US)同步上漲2.83%、收95.68美元，創1月28日以來收盤新高。

無人機概念股走勢強勁。華爾街日報28日報導，知情人士透露，川普政府正與一群無人機業者洽談資金挹注協議，這也是美國政府擴大國內產能、降低成本的行動一環。部分知情人士則表示，至少有幾筆交易可能會透過不同的融資機制、涵蓋債權與股權投資，美國政府將藉此持有相關公司的部分股份。

知情人士透露，在五角大廈列入潛在資助名單的公司中，包括由小唐納川普(Donald Trump Jr.)擔任股東兼顧問委員會成員的無人機零組件供應商Unusual Machines。

Unusual Machines聞訊暴漲57.2%、收29.60美元。其他相關概念股也走高。AeroVironment, Inc.、Red Cat Holdings, Inc.、Ondas Holdings Inc.分別飆漲18.26%、32.61%、22.69%。

ASIC設計服務巨擘邁威爾(Marvell Technology, Inc.) 27日美股盤後 公布 的財測擊敗華爾街預期，暗示用於AI資料中心建設的網路與客製化晶片需求依舊強勁，股價應聲勁揚3.09%、收204.83美元。其他AI晶片股同步跳高。超微(AMD)、高通(Qualcomm Inc.)分別跳漲4.55%、4.24%。

安謀(Arm Holdings plc) ADR聞訊大漲10.76%、收335.27美元，創歷史收盤新高，漲幅居費半30支成分股之冠。Mizuho決定將安謀投資評等維持在「表現優於大盤」，目標價則從290美元上修至360美元，理由是DRAM需求有望續強至2027年，且高頻寬記憶體(HBM)的整體潛在市場規模(total addressable market，簡稱TAM)還在持續成長，兩者皆對安謀有利。

(圖片來源：shutterstock)

news.Title: Intel Has Tripled in 2026. Here's 1 Reason It Could Keep Going.
news.Content: A year ago, Intel (NASDAQ: INTC) looked like a company investors had given up on. Its stock briefly traded below $18, its foundry business was bleeding cash, and rivals had lapped it in the race to power artificial intelligence (AI) . So far in 2026, though, the chipmaker has staged one of the more improbable comebacks in big tech, with shares up about 225% as of this writing. Plenty of headlines have fueled the move. There's a reported preliminary agreement to ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Intel (INTC) shares have risen approximately 225% year-to-date, driven by a potential agreement to make chips for Apple and significant cloud customer deals.
• A key catalyst is the increasing importance of CPUs in the AI era, particularly for inference and agentic AI workloads, playing to Intel's core strength.
• Intel's Data Center and AI division revenue grew 22% year-over-year to $5.1 billion, with operating margins more than doubling to ~31%.
• The company's AI-related businesses constitute about 60% of revenue and grew 40% year-over-year last quarter.
• Challenges remain, including a GAAP net loss of $3.7 billion last quarter and a $2.4 billion loss in its foundry unit, with the stock trading at a high valuation.
news.Keywords: CPU, AI workloads, data center, foundry business, valuation

news.Title: Nvidia Already is the GPU Giant. Now It's Aiming to Dominate in a New $200 Billion Market.
news.Content: Nvidia (NASDAQ: NVDA) has built much of its success thanks to one key product: the graphics processing unit (GPU). This is the chip driving key AI tasks such as the training and inference of large language models, and while other companies sell GPUs and similar chips, Nvidia's have consistently been the fastest and most efficient. As a result, customers have rushed to get in on the company's GPUs and latest updates -- and this ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Nvidia (NVDA) is targeting a new $200 billion market with its upcoming Vera Rubin platform, which includes a stand-alone CPU.
• The company expects $20 billion in stand-alone CPU sales this year, aiming to become the leading CPU supplier.
• Nvidia's GPUs are crucial for AI training and inference, driving significant earnings growth; Q1 revenue climbed 85% to $81 billion, and net income advanced 211% to $58 billion.
• The CPU's growing importance in agentic AI, which involves AI taking actions to solve problems, presents a new growth opportunity.
• Dominating both GPU and CPU markets could significantly boost Nvidia's future revenue and expand its market opportunities.
news.Keywords: CPU, GPU, AI market, revenue growth, new market

news.Title: Micron Surges 850% As It Crosses $1 Trillion Valuation
news.Content: This article first appeared on GuruFocus . Micron Technology ( NASDAQ:MU ) has entered rare air. The memory-chip maker's rally has pushed its market value above $1 trillion for the first time, ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Micron Technology (MU) has surpassed a $1 trillion market valuation, marking the fastest such increase on record (48 trading days).
• The surge is driven by strong investor demand for companies involved in AI infrastructure buildout, particularly memory-related semiconductors.
• Micron's shares have gained nearly 850% over the past 12 months, significantly outperforming the Nasdaq 100 and the Philadelphia Semiconductor Index.
• The company's market cap doubled from $500 billion to $1 trillion in just 48 trading days, outpacing historical records set by Samsung and Tesla.
• Analysts expect accelerating earnings growth, with projected adjusted EPS of $105 by fiscal 2027, a more than 1,200% increase from fiscal 2025.
news.Keywords: memory chips, AI infrastructure, market capitalization, valuation, earnings growth

news.Title: Micron Gets Higher Target From DA Davidson
news.Content: This article first appeared on GuruFocus . Micron Technology ( MU , Financials ) received another bullish call as DA Davidson raised its ...
news.AI_Summary:
• DA Davidson raised Micron Technology's (MU) price target to $1,500 from $1,000, maintaining a Buy rating.

• Increasing reliance on high-bandwidth memory in AI data centers makes Micron's role more critical and potentially less interchangeable.
• Other analysts from UBS, Barclays, and Mizuho have also raised targets, citing strong AI-related memory demand and improved long-term earnings visibility.

news.Title: AI旺 台灣主要IT廠營收增3成、續破2兆、歷史次高
news.Content:
MoneyDJ新聞 2026-05-29 06:17:22 蔡承啟 發佈

即便進入智慧手機出貨淡季，不過受惠AI需求，帶動2026年4月份台灣主要IT廠營收暴增約3成，連26個月增長，月營收規模持續衝破2兆元(新台幣、以下同)大關、創下歷史次高紀錄。

日經新聞29日報導 ，雖進入智慧手機出貨放緩的傳統淡季，不過因AI相關需求持續強勁，帶動供應大量產品、半導體給輝達(Nvidia)、微軟(Microsoft)、蘋果(Apple)等客戶的台灣主要IT廠營收暴增，創下歷史次高紀錄。2026年4月份台積電(2330)、鴻海(2317)等列入「Asia300」成分股的台灣19家主要IT廠營收合計值為2兆1,212億元，較去年同月暴增32.1%，連續第26個月呈現增長，連續第8個月出現2位數(10%以上)增幅，月營收規模連續第2個月突破2兆元大關，創2013年開始進行統計以來歷史次高紀錄(僅低於2026年3月的2兆1,529億元)。

報導指出，4月份19家主要IT廠中有15家營收呈現增加。其中，負責組裝伺服器的EMS廠(電子代工廠)業績持續強勁，鴻海4月營收大增29.7%、廣達(2382)飆增1.2倍(飆增120%)。電源供應器廠台達電(2308)4月營收暴增43.9%。

因AI用先進晶片需求強勁，晶圓代工龍頭台積電4月營收成長17.5%。半導體封測龍頭日月光(3711)4月營收成長19.2%。

另外，智慧手機相關廠商業績不振。機殼廠可成(2474)4月營收大減36.2%，連3個月減少；以生產蘋果iPhone為主的和碩(4938)4月營收下滑15.2%，連7個月萎縮。

台灣企業除了包辦蘋果iPhone大半的生產之外，還掌控全球晶圓代工6成以上市佔率，且包辦近9成伺服器、8成遊戲機的生產，大量供應產品給美中日等全球大型企業，因此從其每月營收動向有望預測未來約3個月的經濟趨勢。日經新聞從2013年1月開始彙整、調查台灣主要IT廠的營收數據。

根據WSTS公佈的預測報告顯示，因AI資料中心投資將成為主要推動力，帶動記憶體、GPU等邏輯晶片需求將維持高成長，因此預估2026年全球半導體銷售額將年增26.3%至9,754.60億美元，將逼近1兆美元大關、連續第3年創下歷史新高紀錄。

(圖片來源：shutterstock)

news.Title: Wiwynn Warns AI Supply Crunch Could Last Until 2027-2028
news.Content: This article first appeared on GuruFocus . Wiwynn Corp., one of the major server suppliers tied to Nvidia ( NASDAQ:NVDA ), is warning that the AI infrastructure boom may be running ...

news.Title: 主動式台股ETF  穩穩賺
news.Content:
受到美伊衝突餘波又起，台股昨（28）日盤中創新高後震盪下跌，跌幅1.4%。投信法人建議，在機會與波動交錯的環境中，不妨透過主動式台股ETF來因應。觀察5月來主動式台股ETF表現，有七檔累積報酬超過一成，三檔漲幅勝大盤。

財富管理專家指出，近期台股表現強勢，屢創新高，不過當股市來到高檔位置時，也意味著資金在各次產業間輪動腳步加快，投資操作上也更需主動調整、靈活因應，主動式台股ETF便是投資人可多加運用的工具。

觀察5月以來大盤上漲12.1%，包括主動群益台灣強棒（00982A）、主動兆豐台灣豐收（00996A）、主動復華未來50（00991A）期間漲幅更勝大盤，歸功於精準選股。以表現最佳的00982A為例，其持股涵蓋晶圓代工、IC設計、記憶體、散熱、PCB等焦點族群，本月來表現相對突出，日前除息後更僅花四個交易日便填息，讓投資人息利雙收。

主動群益台灣強棒ETF經理人陳沅易表示，今年來台股表現亮眼，這波行情並非情緒泡沫，而是具備需求支撐的AI超級循環所驅動，企業獲利持續上修與資金結構性流入三股力量共振的結果。

在產業方面，現階段AI材料短缺問題已從單純的晶片擴大至整體供應鏈，在投資布局方面，可持續關注半導體先進製程、半導體設備、通訊等族群，並往AI應用以及提高生產力族群布局，包括測試介面、光通訊、關鍵材料如玻纖布、CCL、ABF載板以及被動元件等可留意。

主動兆豐台灣豐收ETF經理人王仲良指出，輝達執行長黃仁勳近期談話內容再次驗證台灣在全世界AI全球供應鏈中不可或缺的戰略地位。台灣企業從晶圓代工、先進封裝，到最新的CPO（共同封裝光學）與矽光子布局，各產業鏈正迎來爆發性的成長契機。為了完整捕捉這波AI商機，ETF操作上採取「強核心、耐抗震」資產配置策略，兼具兩大核心亮點。

首先，基金聚焦台積電及旗下先進晶圓代工與封裝供應鏈，同時配置高速傳輸材料、散熱模組，掌握強勢產業的資金熱點。其次，面對黃仁勳提及的記憶體漲價與消費性電子通膨壓力，團隊嚴選具備技術壁壘、產業護城河及產品定價權的優勢企業，例如PCB載板與被動元件，以及電源管理龍頭。

news.Title: 台積電盤後飆2,442新高價
news.Content:
台積電 （2330）股價經歷長達一個月的盤整後，昨（28）日早盤強勢發動攻勢，股價跳空飆上2,360元盤中新天價，市值也一度改寫紀錄突破61兆元大關，最高觸及61.2兆元；雖終場小跌5元收2,295元，但盤後鉅額交易出現新高價、達2,442元。

法人指出，台積電昨天收盤價2,295元，與盤後鉅額交易最高價2,442元存在不小差距，是否意味股價仍有147元上漲空間，值得觀察；而台積電股價漲跌147元，相當於影響台股加權指數近1,200點，在多頭再度作價「仙人指路」效應下，加權指數短線有機會上看44,817點，並進一步向「45K」大關發動進攻。

台積電昨早盤表現強勢，盤中因傳出伊朗攻擊美軍基地，市場憂心中東戰事再次升溫，引發高檔調節賣壓湧現，其中，外資逢高翻臉賣超7,078張，導致股價翻黑收跌5元。

不過，根據臺灣證券交易所資料顯示，台積電昨日鉅額交易爆出歷史新高價與次高價紀錄，赫見20張配對交易以歷史最高價2,442元成交，另有30張以歷史次高價2,369.5元成交。台積電昨天總計出現14筆鉅額交易，成交1,195張，總成交金額27.3億元。

news.Title: 和碩董事長談台股…未見泡沫化
news.Content:
台股昨（28）日大幅震盪1,718點，終場跌620點，並爆出1.65兆元天量，和碩（4938）董事長 童子賢 昨日於股東會罕見對台股發表言論，認為目前尚未進入泡沫階段。另外，外傳輝達有意入股和碩轉投資PCB大廠景碩，童子賢說：「目前沒有這件事」。

童子賢說，從上市櫃公司獲利與本益比來看，目前台股仍具基本面支撐，尚未進入泡沫階段。尤其台灣處在AI潮流中心，現階段發展連30%都不到，看好後續發展空間。

童子賢說明，近期市場對台股是否過熱的疑慮升高，不過，今年首季上市公司獲利逾1.6兆元，若以全年估算並考量下半年旺季效應，目前台股本益比約落在22倍至28倍之間，「還沒有真正超過28倍」。

他說，若與國際市場相比，台股目前仍具備產業與獲利面支撐。以香港為例，香港人口與土地都比台灣小，但港股市值長期以來都高於台股，因此，不該單純因為指數創高，就直接認定市場進入泡沫。現階段台股表現，仍有其合理性與價值基礎。

童子賢提到，台灣目前在全球科技供應鏈的角色持續提升，尤其AI浪潮帶動下，不只伺服器與晶片製造需求強勁，從晶圓、封裝到機櫃等各層級產能，都被國際大型客戶認為相當吃緊，並直接帶著資金投入供應鏈，鼓勵相關廠商擴充產能，也讓台灣科技產業持續維持高度熱絡。

以此來看，整體產業目前仍處於長期成長循環當中，台灣正好位於全球科技浪潮的核心位置，這波潮流短時間內不會結束。

外傳輝達有意入股和碩轉投資PCB大廠景碩，童子賢直言：「目前沒有這件事。」

news.Title: 大神專訪4-1》台股劍指5萬點…還有便宜好股票？前金控操盤手存股清單1次看 兩招買在高點也不怕
news.Content: 台股展現瘋馬行情，一路衝上4萬4新高，劍指5萬大關，眼看AI股個個飛天，隨大盤來到高檔，哪裡還有便宜的股票可以撿，是廣大投資人的最大困擾。前金控操盤手、投資專家「超馬芭樂」王仲麟公開選股秘訣，表示一定要符合近10年獲利穩健、每年都有現金股息發放的公司，這樣即使年年買在相對高點，殖利率仍有不錯表現，耐得起景氣反轉與股災考驗。 長存元大高股息(0056)約18年、合計持有近800張，是王仲麟持有張數最多的標的，其他像是櫻花建(2539)、至上(8112)，手中持股也至少百張起跳，他並透露，今年光是股息預估可領到360萬元。 曾在民營金控任職，38歲從自營部操盤人退休的他說，「以10年為一個區間，我最多只能容忍其中1年沒有配發股利，投資這樣的公司，即使股災來襲，也不會被打回原形。」 年領360萬元股息 以他長期存股的2檔核心標的為例，櫻花建的持股成本極低，約在16~18元上下，因為持有時間長，光靠配股配息，張數就自然增長，除了現金股息，加上配發股票股利，經常還能享受到「買10送2」的額外福利。 此外，0056由於成立時間長，收益平準金約在4.73元上下，配息「底氣」足，即便行情修正，也有本錢維持配息穩定。 至於存股族向來青睞的金融股，他則點名中信金(2891)、台中銀、台企銀、合庫金、兆豐金(2886)等5檔，而在民營金控雙雄部分，富邦金(2881)不同事業體的投資績效優於國泰金(2882)，加上配息較大方，因而是首選。 富邦金優於國泰金 王仲麟分析，資產增值主要有2條路，一是靠股價，像是今年的台積電(2330)，靠股價波動獲利；一是張數增加，隨穩定的股利政策，讓持股部位自然增長，並提醒「不要把套牢合理化」，存股核心應是穩定的被動收入，千萬不要因為想賺價差卻套牢，而更改對底部的定義。 至於如何避開「地雷股」，年輕時曾任上市公司採購專員，卻因公司發生劇變成為「地雷股企業」，被迫負責紓困、下市等流程，因此讓他培養出「閱雷」的深厚功力。 王仲麟表示，可從3大指標判斷，一是進攻型指標（經營能力）：像是毛利率、稅後淨利率、股東權益報酬率（ROE），如果這些關鍵數字不佳，即使口號喊得再好聽，也需警惕。 大股東減持是警訊 其次是防守型指標（避開財務危機）：像是利息保障倍數，可判斷公司是否有足夠能力支付利息支出，以及應收帳款、應付帳款與存貨周轉天數等，都能看出整體的「營運週期」，如果收錢的速度跟不上付錢給供應商的速度，經營就會很吃力。 最後則是大股東與董監事的持股比例，如果老闆自己都不斷質押或減持自家股票，投資人就應格外小心。 不少投資人習慣找飆股，希望在短時間內賺大錢，從不買樂透或碰運氣的他提醒，落底必須靠客觀的具體數據來支撐，而非感覺。 提醒投資時要避免摸底的心態，不要追求買在最低點，而是選擇「右側進場」，也就是待跌勢止穩、出現買盤確認的「勾起來」跡象後才進場，他以進出台積電的經驗為例，雖曾跌到370元，但卻選擇等回升至420元，確認趨勢後才出手。 採定期不定額策略

news.Title: Inflation Just Spiked to the Highest Level in Almost 3 Years in the First Report Under New Fed Chair Kevin Warsh. But There Was Some Good News
news.Content: The Federal Reserve’s preferred gauge of inflation came in at the highest level in nearly three years. However, new Fed Chair Kevin Warsh’s first inflation report also contained some good news. The April reading of the personal consumption expenditures price index (PCE) increased 0.4% in April and 3.8% year over year. Will AI create the world's first trillionaire? Our team just released a report on the one little-known company, called an "Indispensable Monopoly" providing the critical technology Nvidia and Intel both need. Continue » Core PCE, which excludes ...
news.AI_Summary:
• The Federal Reserve's preferred inflation gauge, the PCE price index, rose 0.4% in April and 3.8% year-over-year, reaching a nearly three-year high.
• Core PCE increased 0.2% monthly and 3.3% annually; however, the monthly gains were slightly below economists' projections.
• Higher energy prices, housing, and utilities contributed to the increase, with housing and utilities up 0.6% month-over-month.
• While inflation remains above the Fed's 2% target, the softer-than-expected monthly figures may offer some relief.
• The report is unlikely to significantly alter the Fed's interest rate trajectory, with markets still expecting rates to remain unchanged in the near term.
news.Keywords: inflation, PCE, Federal Reserve, interest rates, economic indicators

These figures are adjusted for non-recurring items. This quarterly report represents an earnings surprise of +4.76%. A quarter ago, it was expected that this video-compression chipmaker would post earnings ...

news.Title: Apollo, Blackstone work on $36 billion debt deal for Anthropic, Bloomberg News reports
news.Content:
May 28 (Reuters) - Apollo Global Management and Blackstone are working to bring in additional investors ‌for about $36 billion debt financing tied to AI ‌startup Anthropic PBC's efforts to expand its AI infrastructure, Bloomberg ​News reported on Thursday.

The debt would be used to buy custom chips from Google. Anthropic would then lease these chips, known as tensor processing units, or TPUs, ‌the report said, ⁠citing people familiar with the matter.

Broadcom, which helps Google develop the chips, is backstopping ⁠payments on the largest portions of the transaction, the report added.

Anthropic said on Thursday it had raised $65 ​billion at ​a $965 billion post-money valuation, ​surpassing rival OpenAI, as ‌the AI startup looks to expand computing capacity to meet growing demand for its Claude chatbot.

The Bloomberg report said that Apollo and Blackstone plan to sell down part of the debt while retaining significant portions ‌themselves.

Investors are being asked to ​submit orders this week, with ​the deal expected ​to close next week, the report said, ‌adding that discussions are ongoing ​and the ​terms could still change.

Anthropic and OpenAI are eyeing potential IPOs as early as this year.

Apollo, Blackstone, ​Anthropic, Google ‌and Broadcom did not immediately respond to Reuters' ​requests for comment.

(Reporting by Prakhar Srivastava in Bengaluru; ​editing by Alan Barona)

news.Title: Famed Investor Stanley Druckenmiller Sold Every Share of Alphabet. He Just Bought 5 AI Hardware Stocks Instead.
news.Content:
With Warren Buffett retired, Stanley Druckenmiller is now arguably one of the most closely watched money managers on Wall Street -- and his Duquesne Family Office just gave its followers plenty to chew on. The fund's latest quarterly filing with regulators, which captures its U.S. stock holdings as of March 31, revealed a clean break from one of the market's favorite names: Druckenmiller sold every last share of Alphabet (NASDAQ: GOOG) (NASDAQ: GOOGL) , a stake he had aggressively built up just one quarter earlier. He also cut the bulk of his Amazon common stock position.

In their place, the billionaire rotated into the unglamorous hardware that the

Will AI create the world's first trillionaire? Our team just released a report on a little-known company, called an "Indispensable Monopoly," providing the critical technology Nvidia and Intel both need.

Continue »

artificial intelligence (AI) boom runs on. The wager seems to be that the next leg of AI spending will lean less on training enormous models and more on inference -- actually running them at scale -- which leans heavily on memory and custom chips. It's a bold move from one of the great track records in the business, so the natural question is whether everyday investors should do the same.

The picks and shovels he bought

Druckenmiller disclosed new positions across a handful of AI hardware names that fall into two clear camps.

The first is memory and storage, where pricing is in the middle of a once-in-a-cycle surge as AI data centers absorb every bit of capacity the industry can produce. He opened positions in flash-memory specialist Sandisk (NASDAQ: SNDK) , memory chipmaker Micron Technology (NASDAQ: MU) , and hard-drive maker Seagate Technology (NASDAQ: STX) .

These companies' growth has been staggering. Sandisk's fiscal third-quarter revenue (the period ended April 3) more than tripled from a year earlier to $5.95 billion, with its data center business alone up 233% from the prior quarter. Micron's most recent quarterly revenue nearly tripled as well, and management guided for current-quarter revenue to grow more than 200%. Seagate, meanwhile, grew revenue 44% last quarter while posting record margins.

These companies are also locking that demand in. Sandisk CEO David Goeckeler called the quarter "a fundamental inflection point" in the company's fiscal third-quarter earnings release, citing a shift toward higher-value data center customers backed by multiyear commitments. And Seagate says its highest-capacity drives are nearly spoken for through 2027.

Story Continues

The second camp is custom silicon. Here, Druckenmiller bought semiconductor giant Broadcom (NASDAQ: AVGO) and chip-design specialist Arm Holdings (NASDAQ: ARM) . Broadcom designs the custom accelerators that big cloud companies use as alternatives to Nvidia chips, and that business is booming; AI revenue jumped 106% year over year to $8.4 billion last quarter, with more strong growth guided for the period it will report in early June.

Why following him may be risky -- and why Alphabet still looks attractive

There's a catch baked into every one of these filings: a six-week reporting lag. Druckenmiller's purchases reflect where the fund stood at the end of March, but the filing didn't surface until mid-May. In between, these stocks went vertical. Sandisk is up several thousand percent over the past year, while Micron has climbed more than 850% and Seagate has climbed about 600%. Anyone copying the trade now is paying far more than he did.

That gap matters because memory and storage are historically cyclical businesses. The forward price-to-earnings ratios on names like Sandisk and Micron look almost absurdly cheap -- in the single digits -- but that is often how deeply cyclical stocks appear at the top of a cycle, when current profits are peaking. Should supply catch up with demand, pricing and margins could reverse in a hurry.

It's also worth remembering how Druckenmiller tends to operate. He has a long history of ringing the register on winners early, sometimes too early -- he exited Nvidia in late 2024 and later called it a mistake -- and he has sounded wary about AI valuations for a while.

Then there's the stock he left behind. Alphabet's business reported a stellar quarter since Druckenmiller unloaded the stock. First-quarter revenue rose 22% to $109.9 billion, its 11th straight quarter of double-digit growth, while Google Cloud revenue accelerated to 63% growth and its cloud backlog roughly doubled to more than $460 billion. Additionally, Alphabet's operating income rose 30%.

At a forward price-to-earnings ratio of about 27, Alphabet is no longer the bargain it was a year ago. Still, this valuation looks reasonable considering the search giant's business momentum.

So should you follow Druckenmiller out of the search giant and into AI hardware? I'm skeptical. His memory and custom-silicon bets may keep working, but they're cyclical, already up enormously, and being mirrored weeks after the fact. Alphabet, by contrast, still seems to offer durable double-digit growth at a fair price.

news.Title: Dell Wins $9.69 Billion DoD Software Deal
news.Content:
This article first appeared on GuruFocus .

Dell Technologies ( DELL , Financials ) landed a major government contract after its Dell Federal Systems unit secured a five-year agreement worth about $9.69 billion from the Department of Defense.

Warning! GuruFocus has detected 7 Warning Sign with DELL.

Is DELL fairly valued? Test your thesis with our free DCF calculator.

The deal covers the Microsoft Enterprise Software Agreement II and is designed to centralize software procurement across the department.

For Dell, this is the kind of contract investors like to see. It is large, long-term and tied to a customer with deep spending capacity.

Microsoft also benefits because the agreement supports wider use of its enterprise software across defense operations.

The deal does not change Dell's business overnight, but it adds visibility at a time when investors are watching demand for servers, software support and government technology spending.

Government contracts can take time to ramp, but they often bring steady revenue once work begins.

The next thing investors will watch is how much of the agreement turns into actual revenue for Dell over the five-year term.

news.Title: Applied Materials Bets AI Boom Will Drive Logic, DRAM and Packaging Growth
news.Content:
Key Points

Interested in Applied Materials, Inc.? Here are five stocks we like better.

Applied Materials says the AI boom is fueling multi-year demand growth in semiconductor equipment, with leading-edge foundry logic, DRAM and advanced packaging driving most incremental wafer fab spending in 2026.

The company’s advanced packaging business is growing more than 50% this year to multiple billions of dollars, and Applied says it is the largest vendor in that category as AI server architectures evolve.

CEO Gary Dickerson said Applied has strong visibility into customer roadmaps, is expanding capacity, and expects continued margin improvement, though China restrictions are now affecting about 10% of the business.

Memory Wipe: The Great TurboQuant Miscalculation

Applied Materials (NASDAQ:AMAT) Chief Executive Officer Gary Dickerson said artificial intelligence is driving a multi-year expansion in semiconductor manufacturing demand, with the company positioned around the areas he described as the fastest-growing parts of wafer fab equipment spending.

Speaking at a Bernstein conference with Senior Analyst Stacy Rasgon, Dickerson called AI “the biggest technology inflection of our lifetimes” and said demand is centered on leading-edge foundry logic, DRAM and advanced packaging. He said those three categories represent “way more than 80%” of incremental wafer fab equipment spending in 2026 and are expected to remain central to growth.

→ Rocket Lab Keeps Making Headlines and Highs—Here's What's Driving the Latest Move

As Tech Earnings Grow, This ETF Still Hasn't Caught Up

Dickerson said Applied is also using AI internally across innovation, product development, operations, supply chain, service engineering and process engineering. He said the technology is expected to help the company bring “multi-billion dollar products to market faster,” including some later this year, and make service engineers more productive.

AI Demand Drives Logic, DRAM and Packaging

In leading-edge foundry logic, Dickerson highlighted gate-all-around transistors, backside power delivery and wiring innovations as important drivers of Applied’s business. He said the adoption of gate-all-around and backside power increases Applied’s revenue per wafer start by about 30% and that the combination of transistor and wiring opportunities can move from $12 billion to $14 billion for 100,000 wafer starts.

→ Quantum Stocks Just Got a Lifeline—Who Benefits Most?

Active ETFs Surge Past Passive, and These Are in the Lead

Dickerson said Applied has built “integration innovators” over the past seven to eight years and that about 30% of the company’s revenue now comes from integrated systems, where multiple technologies are combined under vacuum. He said that capability is important because some films are only one or two atoms thick and can be damaged if exposed to air.

Story Continues

In DRAM, Dickerson said Applied has gained about 10 points of share in overall DRAM spending over a little more than a decade and is “by far the leader” in wafer fab equipment process tools for DRAM. He said high-bandwidth memory requires roughly three to nearly four times the number of wafer starts compared with standard DRAM for an equivalent number of bits, due to stacking and yield loss.

→ 5 Stocks Winning the AI Race While Everyone Watches NVIDIA

Dickerson said Applied is engaged with customers on CMOS logic improvements for higher-speed memory, as well as longer-term technologies such as 4F² and 3D DRAM. He declined to give customer timelines but said CMOS logic is the most near-term opportunity and that 4F² and 3D DRAM are expected to develop “over the next number of years.”

Advanced Packaging Business Growing More Than 50%

Dickerson described advanced packaging as “one of the most exciting inflections in the entire industry,” saying it is critical to connecting computing components at high density and improving “tokens per second per watt,” a metric he repeatedly cited as central to AI computing performance and efficiency.

He said Applied is the largest advanced packaging equipment vendor and that its packaging business is growing more than 50% this year to multiple billions of dollars. He pointed to larger package body sizes, higher I/O density, panel-level packaging, glass substrates and warpage control as areas of customer focus.

“If you look at an AI server today, in three or four years, the way you connect those computing components are going to be very different than what you see today,” Dickerson said.

Visibility, Capacity and EPIC Center

Dickerson said Applied has better visibility than ever into both customer technology roadmaps and demand. He said the company receives eight-quarter forecasts from every customer, along with longer commitments, and that Applied has invested to nearly double its operations capacity.

Applied is also using its EPIC Center to deepen customer collaboration. Dickerson said the company has announced 10 partners for EPIC, including TSMC, Samsung, Micron, SK Hynix, Broadcom, Advantest, Screen and universities, with more announcements expected.

He said the center is designed for “high velocity co-innovation” with customers, including work on future technology nodes extending as far as 10 years out. Dickerson said Applied is working on technologies beyond current N2 ramps, including A14, A10, A7 and A5, as well as future DRAM roadmaps.

Services, China and Margins

Dickerson said Applied’s services business is growing at a high-teens compound annual growth rate and represents a little over 20% of company revenue. He said more than two-thirds of the services business comes from service agreements and prescription or subscription revenue, which has been growing faster than the overall services business.

The company has more than 35,000 chambers connected to AI servers in customer fabs, with the majority remotely connected, Dickerson said. He said Applied is using AI for areas such as chamber matching, predictive maintenance and improving fab output per square meter.

On China, Dickerson said restrictions increased substantially in 2025 and affected about 10% of Applied’s business, more than double the prior level. He said China currently represents the mid-20% range of Applied’s semiconductor equipment and services business and is primarily an ICAPS market, covering IoT, communications, automotive, power and sensors. He said Applied expects ICAPS globally and in China to be flat to slightly up this year.

Dickerson said Applied is introducing new products for ICAPS customers, but noted that leading-edge demand tied to AI is growing faster. On local Chinese competition, he said Applied has performed well where it is able to compete, excluding restricted areas.

Discussing margins, Dickerson said Applied has been increasing prices on both existing and new products, with customer conversations now focused more on delivery times and access to technology innovations. He said he expects the company to continue improving both gross margin and operating margin.

At the close of the session, Dickerson said Applied is positioned in the fastest-growing parts of the industry and has deeper customer relationships than ever. “I have high confidence the growth rate for Applied is going to be very strong, both top line and bottom line going forward,” he said.

About Applied Materials (NASDAQ:AMAT)

Applied Materials, Inc is a U.S.-based supplier of equipment, services and software used to manufacture semiconductor chips, flat panel displays and other advanced materials. Headquartered in Santa Clara, California, the company designs and sells capital equipment and related technologies that enable production of integrated circuits, display panels and materials used across the electronics supply chain.

Applied Materials' offerings include process equipment and factory software that support critical steps in device fabrication, such as deposition, etch, implantation, inspection and metrology, as well as systems for packaging and advanced heterogeneous integration.
